title: P300 dataset BI2015a from a "Brain Invaders" experiment
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalographic recordings from 50 healthy subjects performing a visual P300-based brain-computer interface task using the Brain Invaders videogame paradigm. Participants responded to an oddball stimulus presentation (36-symbol grid with 1 target, 35 non-targets) across three sessions with varying flash durations (110ms, 80ms, 50ms). EEG data were acquired using 32 wet electrodes at 512 Hz sampling rate with real-time adaptive Riemannian Minimum Distance to Mean (RMDM) classification for calibration-free BCI control.
methods_description: EEG recordings were acquired using 32 active wet electrodes (g.GAMMAcap, g.tec) at 512 Hz sampling rate with right earlobe reference and Fz ground. The oddball paradigm presented pseudo-randomly flashed symbol groups on a 36-symbol grid. Three game sessions (9 levels each) employed different flash durations (110ms, 80ms, 50ms). Real-time feedback was provided via an adaptive Riemannian Minimum Distance to Mean (RMDM) classifier enabling calibration-free operation. Resting state recordings (eyes closed) were acquired before and after each session. Data were collected using OpenViBE software with g.USBamp amplifier (g.tec).
